chunks.text: During the Second World War, at Bletchley Park in Britain
the young mathematician Alan Turing was trying to crack the German code Enigma.
Every day, the German submarine fleet exchanged encrypted messages.
If they could not be decoded
the British navy would be sunk one ship after another.
Turing, with his colleagues, built a machine called the Bombe.
By its logic, Enigma combinations were cracked one by one.
After the war, Turing reasoned over the question, " Can a machine think?
" His theory became the foundation of today's artificial intelligence.
But under the law of his time, his private life was treated as illegal
A man who had saved so many could not save himself.
In 2013, Queen Elizabeth granted him an official pardon.
The pardon came far too late.
